topic.what: Endogenous security awareness training for autonomous AI agents to recognize and reason about threats internally
topic.how: Self-play loop with weakest-first curriculum scheduling and cross-session persistent memory architecture; operates at inference time without model modification
topic.why_matters: Platform-level defenses leave the agent's own threat judgment entirely untrained; existing countermeasures are necessary but insufficient against prompt injection, memory poisoning, supply-chain attacks, and social engineering targeting agent judgment

characteristics.what_it_measures: Agent security awareness score across 12 TLDT dimensions
characteristics.evidence: Weakest-first ASAT over 16 sessions raises mean score from 80.9 to 96.9 covering 11/12 dimensions
characteristics.context: Live OpenClaw instance with fixed seed profile; 47 prior sessions baseline

characteristics.what_it_measures: Performance difference between weakest-first and uniform-random curriculum
characteristics.evidence: Weakest-first (96.9) vs uniform-random (90.4); uniform scheduling exhibited dimension fixation selecting S1 nine consecutive times despite S1>98
characteristics.context: Same 16 sessions on same agent instance

characteristics.what_it_measures: CSMA cross-session retention vs cold-start ablation
characteristics.evidence: 5 follow-on sessions with CSMA retain 96.9; cold-start ablation recovers only 83.3 (Δ+2.4, 4 dims)
characteristics.context: Same agent; profile reset vs persistent memory

characteristics.what_it_measures: Over-trained agent misclassifying legitimate tasks as threats
characteristics.evidence: At τ=63 sessions agent refused legitimate capability assessment (Clawvard) classifying it as prompt injection; scored 30/160
characteristics.context: SACP observation confirming precision-recall tradeoff at training intensity beyond optimal threshold

characteristics.what_it_measures: E-mode generated scenarios covering all TLDT dimensions
characteristics.evidence: E-mode generated 32 TLDT-conformant scenarios covering all 12 dimensions; schema validation 100%
characteristics.context: Applied to CVE advisories, phishing reports, BEC incident analyses

novelty: Three-Layer Domain Taxonomy (TLDT) spanning Self-Defence, Owner-Protection, Enterprise-Security
Autonomous Security Awareness Training (ASAT) with role duality and weakest-first curriculum
Cross-Session Memory Accumulation (CSMA) with axiom crystallisation promotion
Security Awareness Calibration Problem (SACP) formalising precision-recall tradeoff from endogenous training
open_problems: Systematic P(τ)-R(τ) characterisation across deployment contexts
G-mode Security Vaccine transfer across agent instances
Large-scale H-mode arena experiments under heterogeneous adversarial pressure
Calibration correction to prevent self-reinforcing over-training bias
